checks.finding: Architecture, ADR-0002, Investigation INV-005/INV-007, and Deployment / CI Plan justify the explicit registry, paired Bash/PowerShell adapters, bounded legacy policy, and atomic rollout.
Design § Open Questions states none remain and records maintainer approval of the registry plan, v1.3.0 revision, and bounded migration.
Design § Assumptions is grounded by Investigation INV-002/INV-003/INV-007 and the investigation baseline at commit 0369c8c.
Design § Constraint Compliance addresses all five Requirements § Constraints: ADR, versioned contract schema, no new legacy full feature, quality-gate-only Done, and synchronized versions.
No performance requirements found in requirements.md.

Design § Deployment / CI Plan names synchronized v1.3.0 plugin/marketplace publication after GitHub Actions OS-matrix validation, rejects feature flags with rationale, and states no secrets, environment variables, service deployment, or database migration are required.

Design § Data Plan identifies the canonical registry artifact, exact per-feature migration treatments and baseline, states existing artifacts are read-only, excludes database/application-data migration, and specifies Git revert rollback.

Design § Integration identifies each repository/CI/gate caller, its purpose, and fail-closed validator behavior; § API / Contract Plan confirms no network API, RPC, event, or third-party service integration.
Architecture, State Model, Integration, Test Strategy, Data Plan, and Deployment / CI Plan cover REQ-001 through REQ-012 and AC-001 through AC-014 without introducing an unrelated product capability.

Design § Security Boundaries and § Test Strategy name traversal, symlink, malformed-input, forged/stale provenance, legacy-bounds, lifecycle, parity, downstream-precheck, run-all, CI-log, implementation-report, and quality-gate evidence paths for the high-risk integrity surfaces.
